compiled_state.title: Evaluate and resolve current TODO/FIXME/HACK and REVIEW-DEFENSE comments
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
The DSO plugin codebase has ~104 deferred-decision comments requiring triage: ~3 TODO-family comments in code (retro-gather.sh, figma_node_mapper.py) and ~101 REVIEW-DEFENSE annotations (~55 in plugin scripts/hooks, ~46 in tests). Some may reference resolved issues, completed tickets, deleted patterns, or unwired functionality. Each needs one disposition: remove (stale), fix in-place (quick fix), or create a ticket (larger work). REVIEW-DEFENSE comments in agent .md files (~10) are instructional and out of scope.

## Success Criteria
- Every TODO/FIXME/HACK/WORKAROUND comment in plugin code evaluated: removed, fixed, or ticketed
- Every REVIEW-DEFENSE in plugin scripts/hooks verified: defended pattern still exists, referenced artifact present, ticket-referenced deferrals checked for completion status. Stale defenses removed
- Every REVIEW-DEFENSE in test files verified against test context (RED-phase tests, non-obvious assertions), not production patterns. Stale test defenses removed
- Walking-skeleton and not-wired-yet defenses checked: follow-on tickets exist and are open, or deferred functionality has landed
- Quick fixes (single-task scope) implemented; tickets created for larger work include original comment text and location
- All existing tests pass

## Scenario Analysis
- Walking-skeleton REVIEW-DEFENSE masks silent no-ops (high): stubs may silently do nothing
- Unwired guard functions false safety confidence (high): removing defense destroys only signal guard is inactive
- active-skill-context reader without production writers (high): defense documents architectural gap
- Cross-story sequencing constraint defense protects gate (medium): removal exposes regression
- TODO stub in retro-gather.sh (medium): orphan metric always reports none
- Test-file defenses treated as production (medium): must verify against test context

## Approach
Single-pass triage grouped by file with separate verification rubrics for production vs test. Walking-skeleton and unwired-guard defenses get enhanced checks. Agent .md instructional defenses out of scope.

## Dependencies
Should run AFTER epic 0df8-1c8c (code quality) and c804-9ccf (error resilience) to avoid double-edit churn on overlapping files.

comments.body: Planning Intelligence Log: Web research: not triggered. Scenario analysis: triggered (6 scenarios surviving blue team). Practitioner cycles: none. Follow-on scrutiny: not triggered. Feasibility: not triggered. LLM-instruction: not triggered. Scale context: not applicable. Fidelity review: 1 pass (Agent Clarity 3, Scope 2 -> addressed via count correction and sequencing). Brainstorm completed 2026-04-16.
comments.author: Test
